trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Atomic dancing, student films and best wishes for break
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 11/24/25

urls.page_title: Universities’ role in society goes beyond education, research | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Americans broadly agree that universities should engage in a range of societal issues beyond their core education and research missions – while avoiding political activism, new economics research finds.
urls.page_title: Portraits honor 8 Cornell faculty as ‘new heroes’
urls.page_summary: The portraits are part of a series by Christopher Michel, the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering and Medicine’s inaugural artist-in-residence.
urls.page_title: Allen R. Carlson | Department of Government
Light causes atomic layers to do the twist | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Using a Cornell-built instrument and Cornell-built high-speed detector, a team of researchers captured atomically thin materials responding to light with a dynamic twisting motion.
urls.page_title: Career Development targets resources for students in tech fields
urls.page_summary: Tech students need to target specific organizations that match their interests, skills and values.
urls.page_title: The College of Arts & Sciences, Cornell University
urls.page_summary: Liberal arts college at Cornell University. A home for exploration, discovery and imagination. Undergraduate, faculty excellence in research and education. Academic diversity in the humanities, biological sciences and discipline-based education.
